zh.Hornby Castle, Lancashire, England: 英國藍卡爾宏比 卡素
zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de la Pole於大約1327年出生在英國越可爾約克時，她的父親Sir William de la Pole26歲，母親Katherine Norwich25歲。她於1344年9月之前與Sir Robert Neville III在英國越可爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1401年4月17日在英國藍卡爾宏比享年75歲。

zh-hans.Hornby Castle, Lancashire, England: 英国藍卡爾宏比 卡素
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret de la Pole于大约1327年出生在英国越可爾約克时，她的父亲Sir William de la Pole26岁，母亲Katherine Norwich25岁。她于1344年9月之前与Sir Robert Neville III在英国越可爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1401年4月17日在英国藍卡爾宏比享年75岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Pole
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Leicestershire): variant of   Paul   or   Poole  .
Americanized form of German   Pohle   or   Pohl  .

variants.name: Poles
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Livio, Primo, Santo.
English and Welsh: variant of Pole and Powell + genitival or excrescent -s.
Italian (Friuli and Veneto): probably a derivative of the personal name   Polo  . It is also found in Slovenia.

variants.name: Paul
meanings: English, French, German, Dutch, Swedish, West Indian (mainly Haiti, also e.g. Saint Lucia), and African (mainly Nigeria and Tanzania): from the personal name Paul (from Latin Paulus ‘small’), which has always been popular in Christendom. It was the name adopted by the Pharisee Saul of Tarsus after his conversion to Christianity on the road to Damascus in about AD 34. He was a most energetic missionary to the Gentiles in the Roman Empire, and played a very significant role in establishing Christianity as a major world religion. The name was borne also by numerous other early Christian saints. It is also occasionally borne by Jews; the reasons for this are not clear. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages and their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Greek Pavlis , Slovenian Pavel and Pavlič (see Pavlic ), Polish Paweł (see Pawel ) and Pawlicki , Assyrian/Chaldean Polous and Polus . In France, this surname is most common in Brittany (see 2 below).
Breton (mainly Finistère): from a Frenchified form of the personal name Paol, Breton form of Paul .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Phóil ‘son of Paul’. Compare McFall .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de la Pole was born about 1327, in York, Yorkshire, England, her father, Sir William de la Pole, was 26 and her mother, Katherine Norwich, was 25. She married Sir Robert Neville III before September 1344, in Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died on 17 April 1401, in Hornby, Lancashire, England, at the age of 75.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret de la Pole est née environ 1327, à York, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Sir William de la Pole, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Katherine Norwich, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Robert Neville III avant septembre 1344, à Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 17 avril 1401, à Hornby, Lancashire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 75 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret de la Pole um 1327 in York, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir William de la Pole 26 und ihre Mutter Katherine Norwich 25. Sie hat vor September 1344 in Yorkshire, England Sir Robert Neville III geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 17. April 1401 im Alter von 75 Jahren in Hornby, Lancashire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de la Pole è nata il circa 1327 nel York, Yorkshire, England, sua padre, Sir William de la Pole, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Katherine Norwich, aveva 25 anni. Lei sposato Sir Robert Neville III prima del settembre 1344, nel Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì il 17 aprile 1401, nel Hornby, Lancashire, England, all'età di 75 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret de la Poleが約1327年にイギリスヨークシュアイアヨークで生まれた時，父Sir William de la Poleは26歳，母Katherine Norwichは25歳であった。1344年9月以前にイギリスヨークシュアイアでSir Robert Neville IIIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1401年4月17日にイギリスランカシュアイアホールンビーで75歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de la Pole(이)가 대략 1327년 영국 요르크실르 요르크에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William de la Pole(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Katherine Norwich(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1344년 9월 이전 영국 요르크실르에서 Sir Robert Neville III(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1401년 4월 17일 영국 랭카실르 호른비에서 75세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de la Pole nasceu aproximadamente 1327, em York, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William de la Pole, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Katherine Norwich, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Robert Neville III antes setembro de 1344, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 17 de abril de 1401, em Hornby, Lancashire, Inglaterra, com 75 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de la Pole родилась около 1327 г. в местности ерк, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir William de la Pole было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Katherine Norwich, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Robert Neville III перед сентябрь 1344 г. в местности еркшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла 17 апреля 1401 г. в местности хаурнби, ланкашир, Англия в возрасте 75 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret de la Pole nació aproximadamente 1327, en York, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir William de la Pole, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Katherine Norwich, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Sir Robert Neville III antes de septiembre de 1344, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió el 17 de abril de 1401, en Hornby, Lancashire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 75 años.
